LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETER FRIEL & FM CONSTRUCTION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. THE GROUNDS FOR A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS "SHALL APPEAR ON THE FACE OF THE CHALLENGED PLEADING OR FROM ANY MATTER OF WHICH THE COURT IS REQUIRED TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE." CCP 438(D). THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF ITS EXHIBITS A, B, D, E, AND F. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBIT C, A LETTER APPARENTLY FROM A PRIVATE ENTITY. THE COMPLAINT IS PROPERLY PLED AND DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHOW IN THIS MOTION THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED. SEE LABOR CODE 3715(A); FELIX V. WORKMEN'S COMP. APPEALS BOARD, 41 CAL APP. 3D 759, 765 (1974). RULING ON DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE (BY OBJECTION NUMBERS): DECLARATION OF JUAN SIMON: OVERRULE: 1, 4 (EXHIBIT ITSELF IS ADMISSIBLE AND IS THE BASIS ON WHICH THE MOTION IS MADE) SUSTAIN: 2, 3, 5 (IRRELEVANT), 6, 7, 8, 9. DECLARATION OF ANGEL BUESO IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION BY DEFENDANTS FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS: SUSTAIN: 10 (RELEVANCE), 11. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-25-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

ATTY. SCOTT CAPLE STRATMAN OF THE STRATMAN, PATTERSON & HUNTER, TEL. NO. (510) 457-3465 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - DEFENDANTS PETER FRIEL, FM CONSTRUCTION & PETER FRIEL's Motion To Dismiss And For Monetary Sanctions Per Ccp 128.7; Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1800 is denied. The court finds no merit on the motion or to award sanctions. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Frederick A. Patterson, Esq. of Stratman Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 510.457.3473, for defendant and moving party;
Juan A. Simon, Esq. of the Law Office of Juan Simon, Tel. 650.366.6476, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs PETER FRIEL & FM CONSTRUCTION's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is Denied. The grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings "shall appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice." CCP 438(d). The Court grants Defendants' request for judicial notice of its Exhibits A, B, D, E, and F. The Court denies Defendants' request for judicial notice of Exhibit C, a letter apparently from a private entity. The Complaint is properly pled and Defendant has failed to show in this motion that Plaintiff's claim is barred. See Labor Code 3715(a); Felix v. Workmen's Comp. Appeals Board, 41 Cal App. 3d 759, 765 (1974). Ruling on Defendants' objections to evidence (by objection numbers): Declaration of Juan Simon: Overrule: 1, 4 (exhibit itself is admissible and is the basis on which the motion is made) Sustain: 2, 3, 5 (irrelevant), 6, 7, 8, 9. Declaration of Angel Bueso in Support of Opposition to Motion by Defendants for Judgment on the Pleadings: Sustain: 10 (relevance), 11. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss And For Monetary Sanctions Per Ccp 128.7; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And/Or Pltf'S Counsel In The Amount Of $1350
Off Calendar Feb-01-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Frederick A. Patterson, Esq. of Stratman Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 510.457.3473, for defendant and moving party;
Juan A. Simon, Esq. of the Law Office of Juan Simon, Tel. 650.366.6476, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs PETER FRIEL & FM CONSTRUCTION's Motion To Dismiss And For Monetary Sanctions Per CCP 128.7; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And/Or Plaintiff'S Counsel In The Amount Of $1350 is denied. Defendants have failed to show that Plaintiff's position is objectively unreasonable. Plaintiff has met the requirements set forth in CCP 128.7. Ruling on Defendants' objections to evidence (by objection number): Simon Declaration: Overruled 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9, 10; Sustain: 2, 8. Bueso declaration: Overruled: 11, 12. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
